subject: Project Management
title: Denver Airport's Baggage System
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Denver Airport's Baggage System.

hook.text: Denver's new airport promised the world's first fully automated baggage system. It was a disaster.

buildup.text: 3,100 telecars on 26 miles of track would sort and deliver bags automatically at high speed.

discovery.text: Bags were shredded, misrouted, and jammed. The system destroyed clothing and crushed luggage.

twist.text: The airport opening was delayed 16 months. The automated system was eventually ripped out entirely.

climax.text: Denver replaced it with conventional baggage belts — the technology it tried to make obsolete.

punchline.text: The system meant to handle bags couldn't handle bags.

quiz.question: What ultimately happened to Denver Airport's automated baggage system?
options: It was ripped out and replaced with conventional belts
It was fixed and still runs today
It was sold to another airport
